headline: OpenAI unveils first chip via Broadcom deal
detail: OpenAI is building its own silicon in partnership with Broadcom, signaling a shift toward vertically integrated AI compute. This directly impacts AVGO — it's a revenue opportunity but also raises long-term questions about customer dependency concentration. Watch how AVGO trades on this; it's your largest position and already in the red.

headline: SK Hynix plans $29B Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10
detail: The world's second-largest memory chipmaker raising $29B on Nasdaq is a massive capital markets event for the semiconductor sector. It could pressure chip valuations short-term as money rotates or reallocates. Your SMH holding is the most direct exposure — monitor for sector-wide ripple effects.

headline: JP Morgan raises S&P 500 target to 7,800, warns of flash-crash risk
detail: The upgrade supports broad market confidence, which helps your SPY, QQQ, and QQQi positions stay stable. But the flash-crash warning is not noise when you're trading on margin — a sudden deleveraging event would hit your concentrated tech book hard and fast. Keep your stops tight and real.

headline: Qualcomm inks deal with AI startup Modular
detail: Qualcomm expanding into AI software and data center build-out signals the chip-to-software convergence trade is alive. Broadly positive for chip sector sentiment, supporting SMH and CRWD adjacent themes. Not a direct mover today but confirms the AI infrastructure narrative your book is built around.
